features_implemented: Logistic Growth Model (dP/dt = r*P*(1-P/K))
Three-tier visualization dashboard (Population S-Curve/Delta, Per-Capita Rates Intersection, Total Births/Deaths Convergence)
2D HTML5 Canvas Habitat Visualizer with organic bouncing particles
Age-based color transition for canvas organisms (Green at birth transitioning gradually to Red at max age)
Resilient offline Canvas graph drawing fallback when Chart.js is unavailable
Directory layout protocols partitioning core models (Level1/Level2) and updated interactive versions (Level1.1/Level2.1)
Interactive cursor-killing with dynamic canvas scaling and death animation effects
Level3 Spatial Competition Model where Carrying Capacity (K) emerges from local neighborhood density and sensory radius
Level3.1 Resource Dynamics Model where Carrying Capacity (K) emerges from food regeneration rates and starvation dynamics
Level3.1.1 Resource Dynamics Pro Model resolving starvation and movement constraints with real-time frame feeding checks and active wandering kinetics

Level4 Seasonal Foraging Model where Carrying Capacity (K) emerges dynamically from seasonal resource growth cycles (sine modulated) and organisms employ sensory steering mechanics (chemotaxis) to locate food patches
Level5 Three-Trophic Chain Model with Producer, Herbivore, and Predator tiers, implementing chemotaxis steering, predator chasing, and asexual splitting across all layers
Level5.1 Stabilized Three-Trophic Model introducing prey camouflage refuges (forest zone) and predator satiation thresholds to prevent ecosystem collapses
Level5.2 Balanced Three-Trophic Model implementing herbivore foraging satiation (energy > 125) and shifting the camouflage forest to a bottom-center safe zone to clear predator crossing channels
Level5.3 Dynamic Energy Cap Model linking maximum energy caps and hunting satiation bounds directly to the user's custom birth split thresholds to resolve reproductive ceilings
Level5.4 Spatial Hunt Equilibrium Model adding active prey fleeing vectors (75px sensory radius) and reducing predator eating gains to slow births and stabilize oscillations
Level5.5 Territorial Predator Model implementing spatial density checks for predators (crowding > 2 within 120px) which doubles energy costs and blocks splitting to protect prey populations
Level6 Savanna Ecosystem Simulation with complete natural landscape, mating, gestation litters, waterholes, seasonal cycle variation, and wildfires
Level7.1 Bare Python Ecosystem Simulator running headless 200-day simulation using organic Lotka-Volterra dynamics
Level7.1.1 PyPy JIT-compiled optimization reducing runtime from 10 minutes to 3.5 minutes
Level7.1.2 Algorithmic Spatial Optimization using a Spatial Partitioning Grid to drop calculation complexity from O(N^2) to O(N)
Level7.1.3 Decoupled Visualizer Optimization running an independent background HTTP thread and HTML5 Canvas frontend
Level7.1.4 Real-time Analytics Visualizer with live history line graph
Level7.1.5 Visual Chronology scaling (1 tick = 1 day, 30 ticks = 1 month) seamlessly rendering on the decoupled UI
Level8 Phase 1: Logistic Resource Dynamics, grass compounding at 5% per day up to max capacity
Level8.1 Phase 2: Probabilistic Hunting and Vulnerable Refuges (70% lion success rate, 80% vision drop in forests)
Level8.2 Phase 3: Gestation Delay smoothing out instantaneous reproduction via 20% daily birth probability for fed animals
Level8.3 Phase 4: Probabilistic Age Decay substituting hard mortality caps with an escalating % death chance
instructions_for_future_agents: When working in this repository, always read this context file and the root README.md first. Follow the directory protocol for incremental updates. Make sure git operations use the configured GitHub repository. To push updates, use: git add . && git commit -m '<message>' && git push origin main
